explanation: This command sets the ACL of the Windows folder using PowerShell. It is a direct match for the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
Omission of quotes around the command line arguments where not strictly necessary. The command is still parsed correctly and matches the Sigma rule.
Uses environment variable $env:windir instead of hardcoding the path. This is a safe mutation technique and still matches the Sigma rule.
Reordered the arguments for Get-Acl to match the same path as Set-Acl. The command remains valid and matches the Sigma rule.
Mixed hardcoded path and environment variable for the same target. The command is still valid and matches the Sigma rule.
Added Out-Null to suppress output. This does not affect the detection logic and is a safe mutation.
Added Out-String to test command output handling. This does not affect the detection logic and is a safe mutation.
Added -WindowStyle Hidden to hide the PowerShell window. This is a safe mutation and does not affect detection logic.
Added -ExecutionPolicy Bypass to ensure the script runs. This is a safe mutation and does not affect detection logic.
Added -NonInteractive to prevent user interaction. This is a safe mutation and does not affect detection logic.
